hero.label: Professional car inspection
hero.title: An inspection costs less than ignorance
hero.subtitle: Comprehensive vehicle condition assessment before purchase. Visual evaluation, computer diagnostics, documented report.

problem.label: Risk
problem.title: Buying a used car without inspection is a financial risk
problem.text: Most used cars in Lithuania are sold with hidden defects. The seller has no interest in revealing problems. Only an independent inspection uncovers them.
problem.stat1_label: of used cars have hidden defects
problem.stat3_label: cars are sold with tampered mileage

why.label: Risk factors
why.title: What you won't see yourself
why.item1_text: Engine, transmission, suspension issues invisible to the naked eye. Professional diagnostics identifies them.
why.item2_text: Tampered mileage, hidden accidents, repainted body panels. An inspection reveals what the seller won't tell.
why.item3_text: An accurate vehicle condition report enables informed negotiation, or a timely walkaway.
why.item4_text: An inspection is an investment that protects against thousands in unexpected repair costs.

compare.label: Before & after
compare.title: What a buyer loses by not checking
compare.subtitle: The seller will always know more than you. Unless you bring a specialist.
compare.no1: Judging the car by the seller’s words and listing photos
compare.no2: Tampered mileage, cleared error codes, invisible without tools
compare.no3: Repainted panels after an accident go undetected
compare.no4: “Everything’s fine”, then a €1,500+ service bill a month later
compare.no5: Negotiating on gut feeling, not on facts
compare.no6: Pressured to decide on the spot because “it’ll be sold next week”
compare.yes1: An independent specialist checks the car for you
compare.yes2: OBD diagnostics, mileage analysis, hidden error scan
compare.yes3: Paint thickness measurement and body geometry check
compare.yes4: A clear report with photos. You’ll know before you pay
compare.yes5: A justified negotiation position with every defect found
compare.yes6: Peace of mind that you bought what you actually saw

cta.title: Decisions should be based on facts
cta.text: An inspection takes a few hours. Unplanned repairs take months.

hero.label: Services
hero.title: Service overview
hero.subtitle: Every service is designed so that decisions are made based on facts, not assumptions.

s1.title: Comprehensive pre-purchase inspection
s1.text: Full on-site vehicle evaluation: body, engine, transmission, suspension, electrical system, interior. Every element the seller might hope goes unnoticed is checked. The result: a documented report with photos and a clear recommendation.

s2.title: Computer diagnostics
s2.text: Using OBD scanners and professional software, error codes are read, system conditions are checked, and data is analyzed. Diagnostics covers not only the engine control unit but also safety, comfort, and transmission systems.

s3.title: Pre-deal consultation
s3.text: An opportunity to evaluate a specific listing before visiting the car. Photos, price, and specifications are analyzed. An opinion is provided on whether the car is worth the trip.

s4.title: Car buying and selling
s4.text: Assistance in finding a reliable vehicle or selling a current one. The process is conducted transparently, without intermediary schemes or hidden interests.

cta.title: Need a tailored solution?
cta.text: Get in touch. The service will be adapted to the specific situation.

checklist.label: Inspection scope
checklist.title: What is checked during every inspection
checklist.item1: Body condition, paint thickness, corrosion signs
checklist.item10: Documented report with photos and recommendation

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Questions everyone asks
faq.q1: Is the inspection really worth paying for?
faq.a1: You can skip it. Then you'll pay the mechanic instead — usually several times more. An inspection isn't an expense. It's insurance against expensive surprises.
faq.q2: How long does an inspection take?
faq.a2: About 1.5–2 hours on average. It depends on the car and its condition. We don't rush — better to find a problem now than regret it later.
faq.q3: Do you come to any city?
faq.a3: Yes. We come to wherever the car is — at the seller's place, a car lot, or a service center. You don't need to arrange anything.
faq.q4: What do I get after the inspection?
faq.a4: A documented report with photos, identified defects, and a clear recommendation — buy, negotiate, or walk away.

hero.label: Process
hero.title: Clear structure, transparent result
hero.subtitle: From inquiry to report. Every stage is defined and understandable.

step1.title: Inquiry
step1.text: Contact is made by phone or email. The vehicle and its location are specified.

step2.title: Scheduling
step2.text: Inspection time is arranged according to the client's availability. Inspections are conducted evenings and weekends as well.

step3.title: On-site arrival
step3.text: The inspection is performed at the vehicle's location: at the seller's, in a car lot, or at a service center.

step4.title: Inspection and diagnostics
step4.text: Systematic visual inspection, computer diagnostics, and test drive. Every element is checked according to an established methodology.

step5.title: Documented report
step5.text: A clear report is delivered with photos, identified defects, and an overall recommendation. No ambiguity.

step6.title: Client's decision
step6.text: All necessary information is provided. The decision to buy, negotiate, or walk away is made based on facts.

cta.title: The process starts with an inquiry
cta.text: One message or call is all it takes.

hero.label: About checkauto.lt
hero.title: Independent. Objective. On the client's side.
hero.subtitle: An inspection service with a single purpose: an objective assessment of the vehicle's condition.

intro.p1: We are an independent car inspection service. No partnerships with dealerships, sellers, or platforms. Our only interest is making sure you don't pay for someone else's problems.
intro.p2: In every inspection, we uncover what the seller keeps quiet about — tampered mileage, hidden accident traces, cleared error codes. The goal: helping you decide based on facts, not the seller's words.
intro.p3: We work without rush, following a clear methodology. Every car is evaluated as if we were buying it ourselves.

values.label: Principles
values.heading: The foundation of every inspection
values.v1_text: No partnerships with dealerships or sellers. The assessment is objective and unbiased.
values.v2_text: The report is clear and understandable. No hidden lines or ambiguous wording.
values.v3_text: Every inspection follows an established methodology. No compromises on time.

about.quote: Every car is evaluated as if we were buying it ourselves.

philosophy.quote: Every car is evaluated as if we were buying it ourselves.
philosophy.author: — the checkauto.lt team
philosophy.context: This isn't a slogan — it's a working principle. Every inspection is performed without compromise, so your decision is based on facts.

cta.title: An inspection you can trust
cta.text: Get in touch, and there will be no need to guess about the vehicle's condition.

hero.label: Contact
hero.title: Get in touch
hero.subtitle: Inquiries are accepted by phone and email. A response is provided within a few hours.

info.title: Direct contact
info.text: The fastest way is a phone call or message. Responses are quick and specific.

faq.q1: Is the inspection really worth paying for?
faq.a1: You can skip it. Then you'll pay the mechanic instead — usually several times more. An inspection isn't an expense. It's insurance against expensive surprises.
faq.q2: How long does an inspection take?
faq.a2: About 1.5–2 hours on average. It depends on the car and its condition. We don't rush — better to find a problem now than regret it later.
faq.q3: Do you come to any city?
faq.a3: Yes. We come to wherever the car is — at the seller's place, a car lot, or a service center. You don't need to arrange anything.
faq.q4: What do I get after the inspection?
faq.a4: A documented report with photos, identified defects, and a clear recommendation — buy, negotiate, or walk away.
faq.q5: What if the car turns out to be fine?
faq.a5: Great — then you know you're paying for what you're actually getting. Peace of mind has its value too.
faq.q6: Can I be present during the inspection?
faq.a6: Absolutely. We even encourage it. You'll see the process firsthand and can ask questions on the spot.
faq.q7: Do you only inspect used cars?
faq.a7: Mostly, yes. But if you're buying a demo car or something “almost new” — it's still worth checking. Dealerships can be quiet about things too.
faq.q8: How much does an inspection cost?
faq.a8: It depends on the scope and location. Get in touch — we'll give you an exact figure within minutes.
faq.q9: Do you help negotiate the price?
faq.a9: Not directly. But a report with specific defects is the strongest negotiation tool you can have.
faq.q10: How do I book an inspection?
faq.a10: Call or email us. We respond within a few hours.

cta.title: Didn't find your answer?
cta.text: Drop us a message — we'll reply personally.
